name: Qualys VMDR
description: Cloud-based vulnerability management, detection and response platform. Yields scan schedule and coverage evidence, vulnerability detection reports, scheduled report outputs, remediation ticket tracking and full detection datasets via the VM/PC API.

steps.action: Log in to the Qualys Cloud Platform, open the VM/VMDR module and navigate to Scans > Schedules; screenshot the scheduled scan list showing scan titles, targets, option profiles and recurrence as scan-coverage evidence
steps.permissions_required: Reader or Scanner (view schedules for assigned assets); Manager sees all schedules
steps.security_note: Use a named individual or dedicated evidence-collection account, not a shared login

steps.action: Navigate to Scans > Scans and capture the recent scan history for the collection period, showing scan dates, targets and Finished status; this evidences that scheduled scans actually ran
steps.audit_note: Pair the schedule screenshot with completed scan history — a schedule alone does not prove scans executed

steps.action: Run a vulnerability detection report: go to Reports, select a scan report template (e.g. Technical Report) filtered to the in-scope asset groups or tags, choose CSV or PDF output, then download the completed report from the report list
steps.permissions_required: Reader or Scanner (run reports); Manager or Unit Manager for all assets
steps.audit_note: Save the report with a date-stamped filename; Qualys stamps the report with generation date and the data time range

steps.action: Navigate to Remediation > Tickets and screenshot or export the ticket summary showing open, overdue, resolved and closed remediation tickets as remediation-tracking evidence
steps.permissions_required: Reader (view tickets for assigned assets); Manager for all tickets
steps.audit_note: Ticket state changes (new, active, fixed, re-opened) are updated automatically from scan results, giving a vendor-maintained audit trail of remediation progress

steps.action: Upload the report exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Vulnerability reports list exploitable weaknesses per host — restrict access to authorised personnel and check before sharing outside the security team

steps.action: Create or confirm the report templates you need (e.g. a Technical Report scoped to in-scope asset tags and severity levels) so the scheduled runs produce consistent, comparable output each period
steps.permissions_required: Manager or Unit Manager (create templates); Scanner where template permissions are granted

steps.action: Navigate to Reports > Schedules > New and create a scheduled report from the template, setting the recurrence (e.g. weekly), output format (CSV or PDF) and report title convention
steps.audit_note: A consistent weekly scheduled report gives a regular point-in-time evidence artefact with vendor-applied timestamps

steps.action: Schedule a monthly Executive Remediation Report (or Tickets per Vulnerability report) to capture remediation-tracking trends alongside the weekly detection report
steps.audit_note: Remediation trend reports evidence that findings are being worked to closure, not merely detected

steps.action: Enable report notifications so completion emails go to a restricted, monitored compliance mailbox, and download each generated report from Reports into the evidence repository on receipt
steps.security_note: Completed reports sit in the Qualys report list subject to your account's report storage quota — download promptly rather than relying on in-platform retention

steps.action: Set a recurring calendar check to catch missed scheduled runs and record any gaps with the reason
steps.audit_note: Unexplained gaps in a scheduled evidence series weaken the audit trail

steps.action: Create a dedicated least-privilege API user: as a Manager, go to Users > New > User, assign the Reader role scoped to the in-scope asset groups and enable the API access permission on the account
steps.security_note: Store the credentials in a secrets manager, never in scripts or pipeline variables, and rotate them on a defined schedule

steps.action: Authenticate to your regional Qualys API server (e.g. https://qualysapi.qualys.com or your platform's qualysapi.<platform>.apps.qualys.com URL) using HTTPS basic or session-based authentication; every request must include the X-Requested-With header
steps.security_note: The X-Requested-With header is mandatory CSRF protection; prefer session-based auth for batch runs and always log out to release the session

steps.action: Pull host-based detection data with GET/POST https://<qualys_api_server>/api/2.0/fo/asset/host/vm/detection/?action=list, using vm_processed_after for incremental collection and truncation_limit to page through large result sets
steps.permissions_required: Reader with API access to the target asset groups
steps.audit_note: The detection list returns per-host QID findings with first/last found timestamps — the authoritative dataset behind every Qualys report; incremental pulls inside a daily schedule avoid gaps

steps.action: Automate report generation: POST https://<qualys_api_server>/api/2.0/fo/report/?action=launch with template_id and output_format=csv, poll action=list until the report state is Finished, then download it with action=fetch&id=<report_id>
steps.permissions_required: Reader or Scanner (own reports); Manager fetches any report
steps.audit_note: Launched reports run in the background without timing out; fetch and archive the output before the account's report storage quota recycles it

steps.action: Collect coverage and remediation evidence: list scan schedules with GET https://<qualys_api_server>/api/2.0/fo/schedule/scan/?action=list and export remediation tickets via the ticket list API (https://<qualys_api_server>/msp/ticket_list.php)
steps.permissions_required: Reader (view); Manager for account-wide schedules and tickets
steps.audit_note: The schedule list evidences that recurring scans remain configured and active; the ticket export evidences remediation workflow operation

steps.action: Schedule the collection script daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline with retries and alerting on failure, and validate each run: confirm outputs are non-empty and compare host and detection counts against the previous run's baseline
steps.security_note: Read credentials from the secrets manager at runtime and ensure pipeline logs never print the Authorization header or session cookie
steps.audit_note: Run-over-run count validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Qualys Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Qualys VMDR has no native outbound webhooks for detections — the closest real capability is continuous incremental polling: run the Host List Detection API on a frequent schedule (e.g. hourly) with vm_processed_after set to the last successful collection timestamp
steps.audit_note: Timestamp-based incremental polling gives near-continuous collection with no missed detections, provided the watermark is only advanced after a verified successful run

steps.action: Deploy the Qualys Technology Add-on (TA) for Splunk with the host_detection data input enabled, streaming detection and KnowledgeBase data continuously into your SIEM for indexed, long-term retention
steps.permissions_required: Manager (Qualys API account for the TA); Splunk administrator
steps.security_note: Configure the TA with the dedicated read-only API account and store its credentials in Splunk's encrypted credential store
steps.audit_note: SIEM retention gives tamper-evident storage independent of Qualys report storage quotas

steps.action: Compute coverage metrics daily: reconcile hosts returned by the Host List API (GET /api/2.0/fo/asset/host/?action=list) against the detection dataset and your asset inventory, flagging in-scope hosts with no recent scan (stale last-scan datetime)
steps.audit_note: Inventory-versus-detection reconciliation is the core completeness metric — it evidences that scanning covers all in-scope assets, not merely that scans run

steps.action: Run automated quality checks and a collection-health dashboard: gaps in the incremental polling time series, hosts exceeding the scan-frequency SLA, remediation tickets exceeding age thresholds, and alerts on any collection failure or threshold breach
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection-health report summarising scan coverage, polling gaps, SLA breaches and remediation trends, and file it in the evidence repository
